model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 training steps on a flat MLP, the current Adam learning rate is likely conservative. Increasing LR should improve early optimization progress within the fixed-step budget with minimal complexity and low execution risk.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The higher LR with Adam degraded validation loss. Switching to AdamW while returning to the prior LR is a low-risk optimizer-family change that often improves early-step generalization for MLPs under short fixed-step budgets.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Under a short 256-step budget, decoupled weight decay can slow early fit in MLPs. Removing weight decay is a simple regularization change that may improve validation loss at this early-training horizon.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The run appears optimization-limited but near a plateau. Increasing MLP depth by one layer is a compact capacity change that can improve representation quality without introducing training-loop complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper network regressed under the fixed-step budget, suggesting optimization difficulty. Reverting to the shallower depth and restoring mild weight decay returns to the strongest known regime before testing new directions.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step evaluator, increasing batch size doubles unique examples seen during training, which can improve validation loss in this short-budget MLP regime while keeping code simple.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The large gain from batch-size scaling suggests this fixed-step setup benefits from seeing more unique examples per step. Pushing to 256 is a direct continuation of that trend with minimal complexity.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Performance improved strongly when increasing batch size, indicating the fixed-step objective is benefiting from larger effective data throughput. Testing 512 is the next minimal continuation of that successful direction.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch scaling has consistently improved loss across 64->128->256->512. Testing 1024 is the next simple step to maximize sample coverage per fixed training step.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After substantial batch-size scaling, the current learning rate is likely conservative. Increasing LR to 1e-3 should better match the larger-batch optimization regime and may reduce validation loss within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Cosine decay over the short budget regressed performance; disabling the schedule returns to constant-LR behavior that performed better in this regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"With very large batches and a short fixed-step horizon, reducing AdamW beta2 can make second-moment adaptation more responsive and improve early optimization quality.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The most reliable gains so far came from increasing batch size under fixed-step evaluation. Testing 2048 is a direct continuation of that dominant trend and may further reduce validation loss by increasing sample coverage per update.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch-size increases have been the dominant successful direction under fixed-step evaluation, with gains continuing through 2048. Testing 4096 is the most direct next step to maximize sample coverage per update.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After strong gains from very large batches, a moderate LR increase is a low-complexity way to better match large-batch optimization and may improve convergence within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At very large batch with only 256 updates, current regularization may be slightly too strong. Halving weight decay is a minimal adjustment that can improve fit while keeping AdamW stabilization.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.75e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Validation remained near the current best, suggesting room for a slightly more aggressive LR at this large-batch setting. Restoring the prior weight decay while nudging LR upward is a focused high-batch optimization test.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.75e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss has continued to improve with incremental LR increases at this large-batch setting, indicating the optimization regime has not saturated yet. A final moderate LR increase is a simple, high-upside test.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.75e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The 2e-3 increase regressed validation loss, so reverting to 1.75e-3 returns to the strongest observed learning-rate setting in this trajectory.","secondary_modes":[]}
